t_ch: …说吧。
t_en: ...Go on.

t_ch: 您也知道，「死亡」的火种始终是「再创世」最大的未知数。无论是凯妮斯，还是您，都希望在接下来的公民大会中，赢得决定这颗火种命运的权利。
t_en: You know that the Coreflame of "Death" has always been the greatest unknown regarding the Era Nova, which is why both you and Caenis desire to gain the power to control that Coreflame in the approaching citizens' assembly.

t_ch: 那刻夏老师，他与凯妮斯达成一致：回收{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的火种后搁置试炼，以求让「死亡」从世界上消失。这样不必继续逐火之旅，奥赫玛也能永享安宁。
t_en: Professor Anaxa has come to a consensus with Caenis — They want to suspend the trial once {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}' Coreflame is reclaimed, hoping thus to eradicate "Death" from this world. If they're successful, then the Flame-Chase Journey will cease, and Okhema can enjoy everlasting peace.

t_ch: 但我清楚，这与您的理想相悖：若不完成「再创世」，黑潮就无法根绝。即便有万敌阁下…黑潮还离我们很远。
t_en: But I know this runs counter to your wishes. So long as we do not usher in Era Nova, the black tide will never be eradicated. Even with Lord Mydei on the front line, and the black tide not posing an immediate risk, the danger nevertheless remains...

t_ch: 实话说，我难以权衡其中利弊…可无论如何，在围绕火种展开谈判前，得有人把它带回来。
t_en: Honestly, it's difficult for me to assess the pros and cons in this decision... But someone must bring the Coreflame before we can begin negotiations over it.

t_ch: 泰坦可能近在咫尺，我无法忽视这个机会…为了挽回{NICKNAME}阁下的灵魂，也必须有人行这一程。
t_en: The Titan could be very near. I cannot let this chance slip by. Besides... someone has to make this trip to bring back {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}'s soul.

t_ch: …说得不错。
t_en: ...Well said.

t_ch: 本来，你就有足够的理由去觐见{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}。只是那大表演家利用了你的愿望…让我们不得不陷入两难的处境。
t_en: You already had every reason to seek an audience with {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}. It's that Great Performer who has weaponized your wishes and placed us in this difficult situation.

t_ch: 没有「死亡」的世界…那也是你的所求，是么？
t_en: A world without "Death"... Do you desire that, too?

t_ch: 如果命运注定要我与您走向对立，我会试着抗争，尽一切可能捍卫我所希冀之物。
t_en: If fate dictates that my path must oppose yours, I will do all I can to resist it, and protect what I aspire to achieve.
Heh...

t_ch: 万敌阁下向我证明了这一点：如果不踏出最开始的一步，往后的一切都无从谈起。
t_en: Lord Mydei showed me that without taking the first step, all talk of what follows is moot.

t_ch: 遐蝶，你变了…
t_en: Castorice... you've changed.

t_ch: （不知道说什么，但微笑。）
t_en: *unsure of what to say, but offers a smile*

t_ch: 阿格莱雅，线下留人啊——！
t_en: Have mercy, Aglaea! Stay your threads!!!

t_ch: …我不会做到那份上。
t_en: ...I'm not going to punish her for that.

t_ch: 蝶，我一直希望你能直抒胸襟。很高兴你这么说。
t_en: I have always hoped to hear you speak your mind, Cas, and I am glad to have heard your true thoughts.

t_ch: 斯缇科西亚，我也曾到访过那里。即便已被冥河围作孤岛，也不难看出它在过去的岁月里是何等恬美……
t_en: Styxia... I've visited that place in the past. Though it is now an island surrounded by the River of Souls' torrents, it's not hard to see its former splendor...

t_ch: 去吧，我不会阻止你。还有{NICKNAME}，身为亲身欺瞒死亡之人，我想你有充分的理由和遐蝶同行，与「死亡」的尊神见上一面。
t_en: Go. I will not stand in your way. As for {NICKNAME}... you are someone who has cheated death. I believe you have more than enough reason to make the trip with Castorice and meet the god of "Death" themself.

t_ch: 我也很期待，遐蝶，当你的故事甄于完整，又将为逐火的旅途新添何种诠释。
t_en: Castorice, I too look forward to your journey. I wonder what kinds of new interpretations your completed story will bring to the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: …谢谢您，阿格莱雅大人。
t_en: ...Thank you, Lady Aglaea.

t_ch: 各位，我无意打破美好的气氛，但……
t_en: I hate to put a damper on this heartwarming moment, but...

t_ch: 既然斯缇科西亚「已被冥河围作孤岛」，我们要怎么才能去到那里？缇宝大人恐怕已经……
t_en: If Styxia is now "an island surrounded by the River of Souls' torrents," then how do we find a way in? Lady Tribbie is probably...

t_ch: 答案显而易见……
t_en: The answer is quite simple.

t_ch: 神话中，{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}曾将亡者的领域作为自己的藏宝地，它能于冥界畅行无阻。
t_en: In the myths, {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#} once made the domain of the dead their treasure vault — that is to say, they could enter and leave the nether realm as they pleased.

t_ch: 若它能够做到…那接替了它神权的半神也至少该有这点本事。
t_en: And if they could do that... then it follows that the demigod who inherited their divinity must be capable of the same.

t_ch: 明日{RUBY_B#午时}践行时{RUBY_E#}，云石天宫见——届时，<color=#dbc291ff>赛飞儿</color>将成为诸位的向导，带你们找到那座古城。
t_en: I'll see you at Marmoreal Palace at {RUBY_B#noon}Action Hour{RUBY_E#} tomorrow. <color=#dbc291ff>Cipher</color> shall be your guide to finding that ancient city.

t_ch: 跌宕起伏的一天终于结束。众人散去，各自整理思绪……
t_en: The tumultuous day finally comes to an end. Everyone disperses, each lost in their own thoughts...

t_ch: 呀，伙伴，辩论会终于结束了？你们赢了吗？
t_en: Hey, friend! Is the debate finally over? Did you win?

t_ch: 优势在我，赢麻了。
t_en: The upper hand is ours. We thrashed them.

t_ch: 咦？可看你的表情……
t_en: Really? But the look on your face says otherwise...

t_ch: 我们和元老院五五开。
t_en: We're tied with the Council of Elders.

t_ch: 喔…那就是胜利在望的意思了！
t_en: Oh... that means victory is around the corner!

t_ch: 我们赢得了一场酣畅大败…
t_en: We managed to win ourselves a glorious defeat...

t_ch: 赢得大败…你还挺乐观的嘛。
t_en: Won a glorious defeat... You sure know how to look on the bright side.

t_ch: 好啦，先不说这个。你参加辩论的时候，人家也没闲着。
t_en: Well, moving on! Hehe, I was hard at work even while you were at the debate.

t_ch: 人家从小蝶那里借了好多书，想试试能不能解决你的问题。现在还差……
t_en: I borrowed a mountain of books from Cas to see if I could find a way to solve your problem. Let's see... I still have...

t_ch: 还差三十二本没读啦！
t_en: ...Only <unbreak>32</unbreak> books to go!

t_ch: 别担心，我们找到办法了。
t_en: It's okay, Mem. We've found a way.

t_ch: 咦？真、真的？那太好了！真是功夫不负有心人…还有有心迷迷！
t_en: Really!? That's fantastic news! I knew it! Hard work always pays off! Especially if you're as diligent as Mem!

t_ch: 人家心里的一块大石头，总算能落地了…呼……
t_en: Phew. I feel like a huge burden has finally been lifted off my shoulders...

t_ch: 呼…突然…好困……
t_en: Why are my eyelids... so heavy... all of a sudden...?

t_ch: 事已至此，先睡觉吧。
t_en: There's nothing more we can do for now. Let's get some rest.

t_ch: 你…说得…呼…人家…先回…呼……
t_en: You're right... Then I'll... go back to...

t_ch: 就像往常那样，迷迷以一种神乎其技的方式，回到了它不为人知的…小窝中。
t_en: As was their custom, Mem, with astonishing dexterity, slipped back into their secret... little hideaway.

t_ch: （晚安，迷迷。晚安，奥赫玛……）
t_en: (Goodnight, Mem. Goodnight, Okhema...)

t_ch: （…现在应该是晚上吧？）
t_en: (...Uh, it IS night time now, right?)

t_ch: 还是再出门逛逛好了。
t_en: Maybe I can go for another walk outside.

t_ch: 人家…要先回…呼……
t_en: Nah, I'm... going...

t_ch: 就像往常那样，迷迷以一种神乎其技的方式，回到了它不为人知的…小窝中。
t_en: As was their custom, Mem, with astonishing dexterity, slipped back into their secret... little hideaway.

t_ch: （无名客的字典里，没有「睡觉」二字……）
t_en: (The word "sleep" does not exist in the Nameless' dictionary...)

t_ch: （…谁也别想让我停止「开拓」。）
t_en: (No one can stop me from trailblazing!)

t_ch: （忙了一天，该休息了……）
t_en: (It's been a long day. Time for a good rest...)

t_ch: 事已至此，先睡觉吧。
t_en: There's nothing more we can do for now. Let's get some rest.

t_ch: （晚安，迷迷。晚安，奥赫玛……）
t_en: (Goodnight, Mem. Goodnight, Okhema...)

t_ch: （…现在应该是晚上吧？）
t_en: (...Uh, it IS night time now, right?)

t_ch: 还是再出门逛逛好了。
t_en: Maybe I can go for another walk outside.

t_ch: （无名客的字典里，没有「睡觉」二字……）
t_en: (The word "sleep" does not exist in the Nameless' dictionary...)

t_ch: （…谁也别想让我停止「开拓」。）
t_en: (No one can stop me from trailblazing!)

t_ch: 晚些时候 奥赫玛某处
t_en: Later, somewhere in Okhema...

t_ch: 阿蝶，原来你也在呀。
t_en: Cas? What a surprise.

t_ch: 缇宝大人…？
t_en: Lady Tribbie...?

t_ch: 有心事吗？
t_en: Is something troubling you?

t_ch: 我想在出发前，和缇安大人再说说话。
t_en: ...I just wanted to speak to Lady Trianne for a bit before I set off.

t_ch: 无论斯缇科西亚过去多么美丽，现在也已是死者的领域…很难说我们会在那里遇到什么。
t_en: No matter how breathtakingly beautiful Styxia once was, the place is now the domain of the departed... There is no telling what we'll encounter there.

t_ch: 我不想…留下遗憾。
t_en: I... don't want to have any regrets.

t_ch: 有人说过，今天你的眼睛明亮了许多吗？
t_en: Has anyone told you that your eyes are sparkling today?

t_ch: 这是…比喻吗？
t_en: Is that a figure of speech...?

t_ch: 以前的你…虽然偶尔也会露出微笑，但大家都能感觉到，那笑容里有一层若有若无的阴霾……
t_en: In the past... When you did smile once in a while, all of us could see that your heart remained troubled even as you smiled.

t_ch: 缇宝大人，你分明知道…那句*明天见*只是一个善意的谎言。
t_en: Lady Tribbie, you know perfectly well... that see you tomorrow is nothing but a white lie.

t_ch: 我不希望你的坚强…只是强颜欢笑的孤独。
t_en: I hope your toughness is not a mask hiding a lonely heart.

t_ch: 除了阿雅，大家都不知道你过去经历过什么，你也很少提起。但我们清楚，那一定是段沉重的往事，就像每一位黄金裔注定要背负的命运。
t_en: Cas... You hardly ever talked of your past. None of us knows what you went through apart from Agy. But we know it must have been filled with pain and hardships, as it is the fate every Chrysos Heir is destined to live out.

t_ch: …很高兴属于你的那颗火种，终于从密布的阴云中现身了。
t_en: ...We are just happy for you that the Coreflame you're meant to inherit has finally surfaced.

t_ch: 我还什么都没有做呢。
t_en: Isn't it a little too soon to say that?

t_ch: 但你已经知道「明天」在哪里了，对吗？
t_en: But you already know where "tomorrow" is, don't you?

t_ch: 不需要依赖任何人，记得全力以赴，别留下遗憾：我一直相信，你的拥抱和别人没有区别。
t_en: There is no need to rely on anyone else. Just give it your all and leave no regrets. We have always believed that your embrace is just as warm as everyone else's.

t_ch: 毕竟「拥抱」的意义就是传递温暖，而阿蝶…是最温柔的黄金裔呀。
t_en: Because we embrace others to comfort them, and Cas... you're the kindest Chrysos Heir we know.

t_ch: …谢谢你，缇宝大人。
t_en: ...Thank you, Lady Tribbie.

t_ch: 这种感觉…不可思议。出发前，我忽然多了许多心愿…想要完成。
t_en: This feeling... It's hard to put into words. I suddenly found myself with so many wishes... so much I want to accomplish before setting off.

t_ch: 那就去做吧，阿蝶！
t_en: Then go do it, Cas!

t_ch: 缇安她…偷偷为你准备了一份小礼物，可惜没能完成。*我*和缇宁正在加工，一定送到你手上。
t_en: Trianne... was actually working on a surprise gift for you. It's a pity she didn't get to finish it, but now Trinnon and I are working on it, and we'll make sure to give it to you.

t_ch: 所以，等你从斯缇科西亚回来，我们再回来这里见面吧。一路平安——明天见！
t_en: So, let's meet back here when you return from Styxia. Safe travels — see you tomorrow!

t_ch: 与此同时
t_en: At the same time...

t_ch: （睡不着啊……）
t_en: (I can't sleep...)

t_ch: *敲门声*
t_en: *Door knocks*
(...Hm? Who could it be?)

t_ch: 希望没有打扰到你休息。
t_en: I hope I didn't wake you.

t_ch: 我这个年纪，根本睡不着觉。
t_en: Sleep is a luxury when you are at my age.

t_ch: 阁下依旧这么有活力，我也心安了些。
t_en: I'm glad to see you're as full of life as ever.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅又要派任务了？
t_en: Is it another mission from Aglaea?

t_ch: 啊，不是的……
t_en: Huh? No. No...

t_ch: 其实，我冒昧登门拜访，是出于一些…私人的心愿。
t_en: I'm here because of private matters, actually...

t_ch: 敢请阁下…听听我的请求。我想拜托阁下陪我去取一样东西。
t_en: If it isn't too much to ask, I'd really like it if you could come along with me to retrieve something.

t_ch: 好啊，出发吧。
t_en: Sure. Let's go.

t_ch: 阁下都不问问是什么吗…？
t_en: ...Aren't you going to ask me what it is?

t_ch: 果然是体力活啊…
t_en: As expected. Manual labor...

t_ch: 不是重物，请阁下放心。
t_en: Not to worry — it's nothing heavy.

t_ch: 我突然变得好困…
t_en: Wow, I feel so sleepy all of a sudden...

t_ch: 啊，难道阁下的灵魂正在……
t_en: Ah! Is your soul...

t_ch: 只是开个玩笑。
t_en: I was just kidding.

t_ch: 啊…你没事就好。
t_en: Oh... then I'm glad you're feeling fine.

t_ch: 精神意志已回归宇宙，再见…
t_en: My will has become one with the universe. Goodbye.

t_ch: 咦，可是阁下的魂息，并没有飘离身体……
t_en: Huh? But I can still sense your soul here...?

t_ch: 啊，原来是玩笑…抱歉。
t_en: ...Ah, sorry! I didn't realize it was a joke.

t_ch: 从这里出发，应该用不了多久。我们…走吧？
t_en: It isn't a long way from here. Shall we?

t_ch: 我要投票！我要投票！怎么投票？
t_en: I want to vote! Tell me how I can vote!

t_ch: 拿着你那枚带有{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}符文的陶片，在心中选择元老院并默默祷言，直到陶片记录下你的选择，再将陶片丢进陶罐里。
t_en: Take your ceramic shard engraved with the {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} rune, select the Council of Elders in your heart, and pray silently until the shard registers your choice. Then toss it into the dolium.

t_ch: 元老院？元你个头！我的家乡早就被黑潮吞没了，还回到黄金世？我看是回到奥赫玛的黄金世还差不多吧！
t_en: The Council of Elders? More like the council of buffoons! The black tide took my hometown long ago! Who are they kidding with bringing back Era Chrysea? It's all a sham to create their own Era Chrysea in Okhema!

t_ch: 泰坦们总是公平的…只要我们不再侵犯它们，就不会遭受神罚。
t_en: The Titans are just... So long as we do not overstep our bounds again, we will not incur divine wrath.

t_ch: 真是个老腐朽。陷入疯狂的泰坦，早该让位于半神。我们走着瞧！
t_en: Oh, please. The time when these deranged Titans should have vacated their spot to the demigods is long overdue! You'll see soon enough, you old fogey!

t_ch: 我倒想教{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}用秤砣砸醒你。走着瞧就走着瞧！
t_en: And I pray that {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#} smacks some sense into you with their scales. We shall see how this turns out!

t_ch: 真是…热闹啊。
t_en: That was... quite a spectacle.

t_ch: 奥赫玛今天也很和平。
t_en: Just another peaceful day in Okhema.

t_ch: 是吗？没有真正打起来，勉强还算和平…吧……
t_en: ...I suppose "peaceful" is technically correct if no actual violence happened...

t_ch: 我也喜欢看人吵架。
t_en: Yeah, watching people squabble is kind of fun.

t_ch: 欸？阁下竟然有这种兴趣吗，那你应该…很喜欢奥赫玛的氛围。
t_en: Hm? You're interested in that? Well, you'll feel right at home in Okhema then.

t_ch: 时至今日，我仍会觉得不可思议。在摇摇欲坠的翁法罗斯，唯独这里依旧是一片光明。奥赫玛的人们还会用投票来解决问题……
t_en: To this day, I still find it almost unreal that Okhema is the only place illuminated by light amidst the crumbling ruins of Amphoreus, and its people are even holding votes to reconcile differing opinions...

t_ch: 这幅和平的光景…简直不像是末日。
t_en: This peaceful sight is making me forget that this is the end times.

t_ch: 这就是逐火之旅的意义。
t_en: This is what the Flame-Chase Journey is all about.

t_ch: 你现在还没习惯吗？
t_en: Shouldn't you have gotten used to it by now?

t_ch: 你的表情…似乎很迷茫。
t_en: ...You look very confused.

t_ch: 在来到这里前，我曾在一座名为「哀地里亚」的雪国居住过很久。那是信仰{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的城邦，以独特的丧葬文化闻名世间。
t_en: Before coming here, I had lived for many years in Aidonia, a city of rime and frost. The people there were devotees of {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}, and were well-known for their unique burial rites and traditions.

t_ch: 而我，曾是那传统中重要的一环。
t_en: ...Traditions that I played a major role in.

t_ch: 因为你的「诅咒」？
t_en: Because of the curse on you?

t_ch: 没错。现在，我是奥赫玛的「入殓师」……
t_en: Yes. You now know me as the mortician of Okhema...

t_ch: 但在过去，我是哀地里亚的「督战圣女」。
t_en: But back in those days, I was Aidonia's Maiden of War.

t_ch: 哀地里亚人在冰原中找到了踽踽独行的我，将我的诅咒视作{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的祝福。他们相信我的双手能终结黄金世的错误，带人们觐见伟大的死亡之神。
t_en: I was trudging across the frosty plains on my own when the Aidonians found me. They saw the curse in me as a blessing from {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}, and believed that my hands could correct the Era Chrysea's mistake and lead them to meet the great god of death.

t_ch: 他们为我提供了归宿，作为交换……
t_en: They provided me shelter, but in return...

t_ch: 我需要对死囚、战俘和英雄们一视同仁，赐予他们「仁慈的死亡」。
t_en: I was expected to grant "merciful deaths" to war captives, prisoners bound for execution, and heroes alike.

t_ch: 是一种临终关怀？
t_en: Is that some sort of end-of-life care?

t_ch: 阁下也是这么想的吗？但……
t_en: Is that how you see it too? But...

t_ch: 这不就是刽子手吗？
t_en: Isn't that just an executioner?

t_ch: 是啊……
t_en: Yes...

t_ch: 在我看来：无论暴烈，还是体面，死亡就是死亡……
t_en: No matter how kind or cruel the means, nothing can change the essence of death...

t_ch: 剥夺他人的生命，从来不是一件值得信仰的事……
t_en: Taking away someone's life is never worthy of religious worship.

t_ch: 我…做不到。
t_en: I... I can't do it.

t_ch: 他们的声音…哀求也好，感谢也罢，全都那样刺耳……
t_en: Their voices... They pierce my ears... The pleading, the thanks...

t_ch: 呵，孩子，我明白。我也曾有过如你这般的迷茫。
t_en: Ahh. I know that feeling, child. I was once as lost as you are now.

t_ch: 但你应当敬重赴死之人。同样地，身为予人以光荣和解脱的圣女，你应当尊敬自己。
t_en: But you must respect those walking towards death, just like how you should respect yourself as the Holy Maiden granting humans glory and liberation.

t_ch: 您要我做的事，根本和处刑人无异，我要如何尊敬自己？
t_en: What you're asking me to do is no better than the task of an executioner, and yet you want me to respect myself?

t_ch: 无论拥有怎样的名目，这都是在夺走他人的性命……
t_en: No matter the titles, I am still taking away human lives...

t_ch: 狂热如哀地里亚的冥军战士，接纳死亡也不在一朝一夕。第一次踏上战场时，新兵往往都会变成逃兵。
t_en: This won't be the first time that Aidonia's fanatical netherwarriors have faced death. On the contrary, recruits new to the battlefield often run away from their first fight.

t_ch: 因为死亡是天地间至高至难的力量。而你能将其掌握，你便配得上这敬意。
t_en: Death is the mightiest and gravest power in this world. The fact that you can wield it means you are deserving of respect.

t_ch: 这…太扭曲了……
t_en: That's such a... twisted reason...

t_ch: …听我讲个故事吧。
t_en: ...Then, let me tell you a story.

t_ch: 为了纪念德拉孔，一位老行刑人，哀地里亚行刑官常用的重剑被称作「德拉孔尼安」。
t_en: In Aidonia, the greatswords commonly used by executioners are called "Drakonian" in memory of Drakon, an elderly executioner.

t_ch: 它的剑身上有三道气孔。如果这柄剑能不偏不倚，迅速落下，三处气孔便不会发出任何声音，受刑者也能在一瞬之间离开人世……
t_en: The sword has three air holes along its body. If the sword is swung down swiftly and accurately, the holes would make no sound, and the executed would also depart this world quickly and painlessly...

t_ch: 但如果有所犹豫，它们便会发出刺耳的噪声。随后，行刑官也必定会听见受刑者的哀嚎。
t_en: But should the executioner have any hesitation, the holes will produce a piercing whistle. On that cue, the screams of the executed are sure to follow.

t_ch: 自德拉孔起，行刑者必须日夜磨砺处决的剑技。他们每一次挥剑都要比上一次更为坚决，以免痛苦滋生。
t_en: Every executioner after Drakon is to hone their sword skills every day in preparation of future executions. Every swing of their blade must be more resolute than the last, for failing to do so will bring pain.

t_ch: 既是为了赴死者，也是为了自己。
t_en: Pain for the dying... as well as pain for themselves.

t_ch: …恕我无法对这种行径感到麻木。
t_en: ...No. I can never get used to this.

t_ch: 不，孩子。我不想你对此习以为常。
t_en: You misunderstand, child. I am not telling you to get used to it.

t_ch: 我只要你思考死者为何而痛苦。然后让你的每一次触碰，都比上一次更加坚决。
t_en: I'm asking you to consider the source of the dead's suffering, and use that knowledge to fortify your future touches with more and more determination.

t_ch: 与死者同行，敬爱他们如同你敬爱生命…你将再也听不见那些杂音。
t_en: Go walk with the dead, and love them as you love life... Then you'll no longer hear those noises.

t_ch: 别杀我…求求你，我不想死……
t_en: Please... Don't kill me... I don't want to die....

t_ch: <color=#eb4d3dff>轻轻触碰。</color>
t_en: <color=#eb4d3dff>A gentle touch.</color>

t_ch: 不，我做不到。
t_en: No. I can't do it.

t_ch: 这是你的义务。记住，思考死者为何而痛苦，然后向他们献上敬意。
t_en: This is your duty. Remember, consider the source of the dead's suffering, then give them the respect they deserve.

t_ch: 谢、谢谢你…别听她的，求求你……
t_en: Th—Thank you... I beg you... Don't listen to her...

t_ch: <color=#eb4d3dff>轻轻触碰。</color>
t_en: <color=#eb4d3dff>A gentle touch.</color>

t_ch: 不…不行…
t_en: I—I can't...

t_ch: 若你无法学会接纳死亡，便不能为哀地里亚所接纳。
t_en: If you cannot learn to accept death, then Aidonia has no place for you.

t_ch: 这是最后一次机会：动手。
t_en: This is your last chance. Do it.

t_ch: 不…我不想死…求求你了，求求你……
t_en: No, please... I don't want to die... Please, I'm begging you...

t_ch: <color=#eb4d3dff>轻轻触碰。</color>
t_en: <color=#eb4d3dff>A gentle touch.</color>

t_ch: 不、不……
t_en: No... No...

t_ch: 你用手指轻触囚犯的额头。他面色苍白，目光闪烁，眼中满是惊恐，身体止不住地痉挛。
t_en: You gently touch the prisoner's forehead with your finger. His face is pale, his eyes flicker with fear, and his body trembles uncontrollably.

t_ch: 因为恐惧、愤怒、憎恨，还是你所谓*仁慈的死亡之触*根本就是个谎言？
t_en: Is it because of fear, anger, and hatred, or is your so-called merciful Touch of Death simply a lie?

t_ch: 黑紫色的淡斑如同花瓣，在死囚的脸上舒展，随后又仿佛泉水淹没身体。他的震颤停止了，方才止不住求饶的呼喊声逐渐变为似叹息的呜咽，兀自咕哝着。
t_en: The dark purple mottles spread like petals across the death row inmate's face, then seemed to be submerged by springwater, flooding his body. His trembling ceased, and the cries that had once begged for mercy slowly turned into a sigh-like whimper, as he murmurs to himself.

t_ch: 很快，一切陷入死寂，化作漆黑的露水随风散去。你听到自己的心跳盖过剧烈的呼吸声。
t_en: Quickly, everything falls into a dead silence, turning into pitch-black dew that scatters with the wind. You hear your own heartbeat drown out the sound of heavy breathing.

t_ch: 希望斯人牺牲能令你有所感悟。
t_en: May you gain some insights through his sacrifice.

t_ch: 这样…就可以了吗？
t_en: Is... this all?

t_ch: 不，跟我来。
t_en: No. Come with me.

t_ch: 作为「督战圣女」，你的人生才刚刚开始。
t_en: This is just the beginning of your life as the Maiden of War.

t_ch: …您这是要我做什么？
t_en: ...What are you trying to do with me?

t_ch: 我老了，必须响应{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的召唤，去往它的国度了……
t_en: I have aged... The time has come for me to heed the summons of {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} and journey to their domain.

t_ch: 这…也是每一个哀地里亚人的归宿。
t_en: ...The final resting place of every Aidonian.

t_ch: 您还远没有衰老到那种地步，还有许多岁月可活……
t_en: You're not that old yet... You still have many, many years ahead of you...

t_ch: 不…我已经「死」了。
t_en: No no... I'm already "dead."

t_ch: 我对加诸此身的衰老，还有临近的死亡…开始感到恐惧了。
t_en: For I have come to fear... my declining health and impending death.

t_ch: 所以，遐蝶，你能给我最后一次机会，让我向那恐惧发起冲锋，证明自己并非懦夫。
t_en: Castorice, will you give me one last chance to charge towards fear and prove that I am no coward?

t_ch: 我…再也受不了了……
t_en: I... I can't take this anymore...

t_ch: 孩子，是我从冰原中带回了你，令你荒唐的命运开始转动。身为哀地里亚的行刑官之首，也是我将「处刑」这道重任不由分说，强压在你肩头。
t_en: Child, it was I who brought you here from the frosty plains and set your farcical fate in motion. It was also I, chief executioner of Aidonia, who forced the heavy duty of executing others onto you.

t_ch: 我深知，你始终都对我…对{RUBY_B#哀地里亚人}我们{RUBY_E#}信仰的一切感到困惑，甚至愤怒……
t_en: I've always known... that you have doubts and even anger about {RUBY_B#Aidonian}my{RUBY_E#} faith... and about me...

t_ch: 我…别无选择……
t_en: I... never had a choice...

t_ch: 是啊，我们的所作所为与绑架无异。
t_en: Indeed. We essentially abducted you.

t_ch: 所以，只要你轻轻一触，一条罪恶的生命便将就此凋零。逃离对你而言，不是易如反掌？
t_en: Yet you can end any sinful life with just a touch of your fingers... Shouldn't it be easy for you escape from here?

t_ch: 我…不可能这么做。
t_en: ...I'll never do that.

t_ch: 就算哀地里亚人再怎么不加思考，视死亡如儿戏，但…我做不到。
t_en: It doesn't matter how lightly the Aidonians take death, or how readily they accept it... I simply can't do it.

t_ch: 呵呵，所以…我果然没看错你。
t_en: Haha... I knew I was right about you.

t_ch: 什么…？
t_en: Wh—What do you mean?

t_ch: 我一直把你当成女儿，悉心培养，因为你生来受赐如此{RUBY_B#诅咒}祝福{RUBY_E#}，这世上没有人比你更明白生命和死亡的重量。
t_en: I've taught you and cared for you as my own daughter all this time... because of this {RUBY_B#curse}blessing{RUBY_E#} that's been with you since your birth. I know no one else in the world would understand the weight of life and death more than you do.

t_ch: 若用哀地里亚人的眼光看待「死亡」：它是一切生命的终点，是我们每个人的必由之路。人不应抗拒，而要学会如何迎接它。
t_en: In the eyes of an Aidonian, "Death" is the end of all life, a destination we will all eventually reach. Humans should not resist it, but learn how to face it.

t_ch: 只有在彻悟这个道理后，你才能好好活下去。
t_en: You can only live your life to the fullest after understanding this principle.

t_ch: 哪怕你终将离开哀地里亚的庇护——哪怕永远肩负着{RUBY_B#祝福}诅咒{RUBY_E#}——你也可以像普通人一样，昂首挺胸地活下去。
t_en: And when you do, even if you eventually leave Aidonia's protection... even if you must carry the {RUBY_B#blessing}curse{RUBY_E#} forever... you can live with your head held high, like everyone else.

t_ch: 不，我怎么能……
t_en: No, how can I—

t_ch: 来，动手吧。我已教给你我此生所能领悟的一切……
t_en: Come now. It's time. I have already taught you all that I know...

t_ch: 证明自己是个合格的圣女，或是具备如此决心吧。
t_en: Show me that you are a fitting Holy Maiden... or at least, that you possess the resolve to become one.

t_ch: 我最后一次服从她的指示，轻轻触碰了眼前的老人。
t_en: This was the last time I followed her command. I gently touched the elderly woman before me.

t_ch: 这位见惯了死亡的处刑人，她的消逝与常人并无不同：喉头的呜咽，眼中的遗憾，还有…微不可见的颤抖从指尖传来。
t_en: The executioner who had seen too much death died the same way as everyone else... A gasp for air, a wistful look in her eyes, and a barely perceptible tremor in her fingertips.

t_ch: 阿蒙内特，试图向我传授「死亡」的人，也最终如朝露消失在晨雾中。她一生都在贯彻哀地里亚人的信仰，而当死亡真正来临，她也如那信仰所述……
t_en: Elder Amunet, the person who tried to teach me about "Death," ultimately vanished like dew in the morning mist. She spent her life embodying the beliefs of the Aidonians, and when death finally came, she embraced it exactly as their doctrine had taught...

t_ch: 除去敬畏，再无其他。
t_en: I have nothing but respect for her.

t_ch: 我在哀地里亚城外的沼泽间，为她树立了一座衣冠冢，将她留在我亲手送葬的一众死者间。然后，为了觐见{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}，我再次踏上了流浪。
t_en: I erected a cenotaph in her honor in the marshes outside Aidonia's city walls with the others I had laid to rest. Then, I set out once more in search of {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 那是一段很长、很长的路，我听过太多对死亡的议论。「生命从死亡中寻获意义」、「万物因终结而前进」……
t_en: It was a long, long journey... I heard so many discussions about death along the way. "Life finds meaning in death." "The world improves because there is an end"...

t_ch: 仿佛世间最可怕的幽暗，所有人都在讲述它的沉重，或是抵抗、或是崇拜、或是敬畏……
t_en: It seems the most terrible darkness in this world. Everyone talks about its heavy weight. Some resist it, some worship it, and some even revere it...

t_ch: 一尊去向不明的泰坦，何以成为人们心中最深的恐惧……
t_en: How did a disappeared Titan become the deepest fear of humankind...

t_ch: …为何，会在我的梦中萦绕不去？
t_en: ...And why do they haunt my dreams?

t_ch: 为了这个问题的答案，我继续旅途。起初是逃离，而后变成了寻索，最后……
t_en: I continued my travels to seek an answer. Initially, I was running away. Then, I started searching for it. And now, at the end...

t_ch: 我想知道，是否，我的触碰，我的拥抱…并非只能剥夺……
t_en: I just want to know if my touch... if my embrace... can do more than taking things away...

t_ch: 而是也可以，留下些什么？
t_en: Can I make anything...? Can I leave anything behind?

t_ch: {RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}试炼完成的那一天，{NICKNAME}阁下一定也很困惑吧？
t_en: On the day that {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx's{RUBY_E#} Trial ended, you must have had many questions, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 因为我也是：我不明白，为什么阁下身为天外之人，会以这种残忍的方式早早卷入翁法罗斯的命运……
t_en: I felt just as confused. Why, as someone from beyond the sky, were you so cruelly dragged into Amphoreus's fate?

t_ch: …我想做些什么，可即便尽力表现地更自然些，也无法像普通人那样，理所当然地与生者相碰。
t_en: ...I wished I could take action, but no matter how hard I try, I cannot simply interact with the living as ordinary people do.

t_ch: 哪怕早在树庭，我们就有过无心的接触。那时明明什么都没有发生……
t_en: Even when we brushed against each other unintentionally in the Grove... and it was obvious that nothing had happened...

t_ch: 当我心中终于涌起近乎奢望的希冀……
t_en: When an almost unattainable hope finally welled up in my heart...

t_ch: 当我以为自己的触碰，或许不会再带来悲伤……
t_en: When I thought that perhaps my touch would no longer bring sorrow...

t_ch: 可命运却要告诉我，不是「死亡」的诅咒终于可以治愈……
t_en: Fate declared to me: It wasn't that the curse of "Death" had finally been lifted...

t_ch: 而是阁下的灵魂不再属于人间…我的双手早已夺无可夺，仅此而已么？
t_en: ...but that your soul no longer belonged to this world... and there was nothing left for my hands to take. Nothing.

t_ch: 是啊，难道这一次就会有所改变吗？我不是不明白，只是不愿意接受现实罢了。
t_en: And did I really think this time would be any different? It's not that I don't understand this reality — just that I'm unwilling to accept it.

t_ch: 明明都走过了如此漫长的旅途，我仍找寻不到生与死的答案，永远无可奈何……
t_en: Having walked such a long road, I am still unable to find the answer to life and death. I am resigned to eternal despair...

t_ch: 想要留下些什么，也只能任由「死亡」将它们从指缝间带走……
t_en: Anything I wish to hold on to, "Death" will inevitably wrest from my grasp...

t_ch: 可在那之后，我甚至都没有再次确认的勇气…直到{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}剖白残酷的事实，带来你我面前。
t_en: And from that moment on, I lacked the courage to confirm it again... Until {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#} laid bare the cruel truth before us.

t_ch: 遐蝶和你谈起她送给其他黄金裔的礼物，不知不觉就走到「你的礼物」前。
t_en: Castorice talks to you about the gifts she gave to other Chrysos Heirs, and before you know it, she arrives at "your gift."

t_ch: 但，现在…或许我还有最后一次机会。
t_en: But now... perhaps I might have one last chance.

t_ch: 很久以前，缇宁老师为我解读过一则预言。她说：<color=#dbc291ff>「花海尽头，生者的魂灵将温暖汝之指尖；相拥之后，便是永恒的离别。」</color>
t_en: Long ago, Trinnon read a prophecy to me: <color=#dbc291ff>"At the end of the sea of flowers, the souls of the living shall warm thy fingertips, and after an embrace... there shall be eternal separation."</color>

t_ch: 很高兴你能对我坦诚相见。
t_en: Thank you for telling me your thoughts.

t_ch: 这样…也算是不留遗憾了吧。
t_en: With that... I won't have any regrets.

t_ch: 呃…这些需要我当没听到吗？
t_en: ...Should I pretend I didn't hear any of that?

t_ch: 啊，我……
t_en: Ah! I...

t_ch: 抱歉，说太多了…希望没有让阁下感到困扰。
t_en: I'm sorry. I probably said too much... I hope I didn't make you uncomfortable.

t_ch: 先不说这个了。我们到了，阁下。
t_en: Let's put that aside for the moment. We've arrived.

t_ch: 想要阁下陪我取的东西，是我为你准备的一件小礼物，手工制作的，就像先前送给缇安大人的饰品…我想给大家都留下一份心意。
t_en: I want to retrieve a small gift I specially prepared for you. I made it myself, like the trinket I gave Lady Trianne... I want to leave something for everyone, if possible.

t_ch: 觐见「死亡」泰坦，此行恐怕也前途未卜…我想，要是能抓住临行前的每一刻，不留遗憾就好了。
t_en: The journey to seek an audience with the Death Titan cannot be an easy one... I want to make full use of every moment I have before we depart, so that I don't have any regrets.

t_ch: 是我的大头胸针吗？
t_en: Is it a brooch with my face on it?

t_ch: 不是，阁下一见便知。
t_en: No, it isn't. You'll know when you see it.

t_ch: 是不是有点不吉利…
t_en: That sounds kind of ominous...

t_ch: 这…还请阁下先过目，如果不喜欢，拒绝也不迟。
t_en: Then... Perhaps you can have a look at it first? You don't have to take it if you don't like it.

t_ch: 看，就是它……
t_en: Look, it's right there—
...Huh?

t_ch: 礼物…不见了？！
t_en: Where... did it go?

t_ch: 对此我并不惊讶，因为她说的仿佛并非预言，而是我过去的全部写照。
t_en: I found no surprise in it, for her words sounded not like a foretelling, but a perfect reflection of my past.

t_ch: 世上的拥抱本该为相逢而生，可我的双手只能诉说告别。那时的我也有理由相信，这道宿命终将贯穿我的一生……
t_en: Embraces in this world are born of reunions, yet my touch speaks only of farewells. In that moment, I had no doubt that this destiny would weave itself through the entirety of my life...

t_ch: 可现在事情迎来了转机：既然终于能够觐见{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}…那我就还有机会为那注定的预言，写下另一种诠释。
t_en: And yet things have taken a turn for the better: Now that I can finally have an audience with {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}, perhaps I can rewrite the meaning of that fated prophecy.

t_ch: 我没有理由不倾尽全力……
t_en: I have no reason not to give it my all...

t_ch: …为了阁下，也为了我自己。
t_en: ...for your sake, and for my own.

t_ch: 这…怎么会这样……
t_en: What's... going on...?

t_ch: 好像有人留下了什么。
t_en: Looks like someone left something behind.

t_ch: 这是……
t_en: This is a...

t_ch: 不会是很贵重的东西吧…
t_en: I hope the gift wasn't expensive...

t_ch: 只是一些手工制品，按理说不会被窃贼盯上才对……
t_en: Just some handcrafted trinkets... Things that shouldn't catch the eye of thieves anyways...

t_ch: 咦？这是……
t_en: Hmm? What's this...

t_ch: 你惹上什么神秘怪盗团了？
t_en: Did you get into trouble with some phantom thieves?

t_ch: 我、我也不是什么腐败的大人吧……
t_en: I—I'm not some corrupt grown-up...

t_ch: 咦？这是……
t_en: Hmm? What's this...

t_ch: {RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}的硬币，还有这块石板……
t_en: The coin of {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#}, and a slate...

t_ch: 果然是她。
t_en: So it's her.

t_ch: 谁？
t_en: Who?

t_ch: 我了个猫咪怪盗啊…
t_en: Holy moly, a kitty thief?

t_ch: 不会错，这就是她最爱用的象征。
t_en: No doubt about it. This is her favorite symbol.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>赛飞儿</color>阁下，阿格莱雅大人早先说过的，{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}的半神，在翁法罗斯各地留下过神话传说的飞贼……
t_en: It's Lady <color=#dbc291ff>Cipher</color>, the demigod of {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#} whom Lady Aglaea had spoken of. She's a master thief who's left legendary tales all over Amphoreus.

t_ch: 好久不见，她的登场方式依旧这么…「恶劣」。
t_en: So many years have passed, yet she's still announcing herself in such a... "crass" manner.

t_ch: 阁下，我们先按石板上说的，找到赛飞儿阁下吧。
t_en: Let's just do as the slate says and look for Lady Cipher.

t_ch: 为了取回礼物，也为给猫咪怪盗一点教训，你们动身前往市集，并找到了她留下的短矛。
t_en: To retrieve the gift and teach Kitty Phantom Thief a lesson, you head to the market and find the short spear she left behind.

t_ch: …这是她留下的诡计。
t_en: ...This is a trick she left for us.

t_ch: 我们追。
t_en: We must pursue her.

t_ch: 这是假的。
t_en: This is the wrong one.

t_ch: 这才是对的。
t_en: This is the right one.

t_ch: …确实是她会做的事。
t_en: ...Exactly the kind of thing she would do.

t_ch: 这就是怪盗信中提到的「黄金短矛」。
t_en: This is the "Golden Short Spear" mentioned in the thief's letter.

t_ch: 似乎由于某种力量限制，它无法被修复。
t_en: Due to some kind of power restriction, it cannot be repaired.

t_ch: …这是她留下的诡计。
t_en: ...This is a trick she left for us.

t_ch: 很显然，猫咪怪盗想和你玩一个小游戏——两根短矛，一个正确，一个错误。
t_en: Clearly, Kitty Phantom Thief wants to play a little game with you — two short spears, one correct, one wrong.

t_ch: 我看看卷轴上的提示。
t_en: Let me check the hint on the scroll.

t_ch: 两根黄金短矛
t_en: Two Golden Short Spears

t_ch: 只有刻着真话的黄金短矛能带你们找到猫咪怪盗。
t_en: Only the Golden Short Spear engraved with the truth can lead you to Kitty Phantom Thief.

t_ch: 查看左边的黄金短矛。
t_en: Inspect the left Golden Short Spear.

t_ch: 左边的短矛上刻着字——你别被耍了，右边的短矛刻着假话。
t_en: The left spear is engraved with the words: "Don't be fooled, the right spear is engraved with lies."

t_ch: 查看右边的黄金短矛。
t_en: Inspect the right Golden Short Spear.

t_ch: 右边的短矛上刻着字——相信我灰子，这两根短矛都刻着真话。
t_en: The right spear is engraved with the words: "Trust me, Gray Mystery, both spears are engraved with the truth."

t_ch: 我已经想明白了。
t_en: I've figured it out.

t_ch: 卷轴上的话应该是真的，那么你想修复的是哪一根短矛呢？
t_en: The words on the scroll should be true, so which short spear do you want to repair?

t_ch: 修复左边的黄金短矛。
t_en: Repair the left Golden Short Spear.

t_ch: 修复右边的黄金短矛。
t_en: Repair the right Golden Short Spear.

t_ch: 真是性格顽劣…阁下，跟我来！
t_en: Ugh, she's incorrigible... Follow me!

t_ch: 这根短矛飞了一圈回到了原地，又若无其事地假装自己是一道新的题目。
t_en: The short spear flew in a circle and returned to its original spot, nonchalantly pretending to be a new puzzle.

t_ch: …确实是她会做的事。
t_en: ...Exactly the kind of thing she would do.

t_ch: 修复左边的黄金短矛。
t_en: Repair the left Golden Short Spear.

t_ch: 这才是对的。
t_en: This is the right one.

t_ch: 修复右边的黄金短矛。
t_en: Repair the right Golden Short Spear.

t_ch: 这是假的。
t_en: This is the wrong one.

t_ch: 你注意到遐蝶并不想浪费时间，便直接修复了左边的短矛。
t_en: You notice Castorice doesn't want to waste time, so you immediately repair the left spear.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们追。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, we must pursue her.

t_ch: 呜嗡嗡……
t_en: Grumph...

t_ch: 赛飞儿——如果这头大地兽真的是她——那么她看起来似乎十分惬意，眼神里饱含三分真诚、三分赞许、三分得逞，和三分「哈哈，灰子，认真你就输了」。
t_en: Cipher — if this dromas truly is her — appears to be quite content, her gaze filled with three parts sincerity, three parts approval, three parts triumph, and three parts "Hahah, Gray Mystery, you've lost if you take this seriously."

t_ch: 你好，赛飞儿。
t_en: Hi, Cipher.

t_ch: 嗡嗡……
t_en: Grumph...

t_ch: 她似乎在说：「你好。」
t_en: She seems to be saying, "Hello."

t_ch: 见到你对待大地兽也如此郑重，她总计十二分的复杂情感立刻转变为十分佩服。
t_en: Seeing you treat the dromas with such seriousness, all twelve parts of her complex emotions immediately transformed into one of admiration.

t_ch: 原来大地兽就是猫咪。
t_en: I see. So a dromas is a cat.

t_ch: 嗡嗡……
t_en: Grumph...

t_ch: 她似乎在说：「对的，哦不对，对的对的，对的。」
t_en: She seems to be saying, "Right. Oh, no, yeah yeah, yes."

t_ch: 她在一阵自我拉扯中得出了肯定的结论：大地兽确实就是猫咪。
t_en: After a moment of inner conflict, she reached a definitive conclusion: The dromas is indeed a cat.

t_ch: 再说一遍，你管我叫什么？
t_en: What did you just call me!?

t_ch: 嗡嗡……
t_en: Grumph...

t_ch: 她似乎在说：「啊哈哈，赛飞儿不知道哦。」
t_en: She seems to be saying, "Ah hahah, Cipher does not know."

t_ch: 啊这，她说她不知道。
t_en: Hmm, so. She says she doesn't know.

t_ch: …赛飞儿阁下，请出来吧。
t_en: ...Lady Cipher, please come on out.

t_ch: 你呀，还是这么不小心。重要的礼物，不该揣在怀里小心护着吗？
t_en: Tsk. You're still careless as ever. Shouldn't you keep such a precious gift near your bosom?

t_ch: 既然你知道这是一件礼物，就请还给我吧。不要混在人群里千里传声了。
t_en: Since you know it's a gift for someone else, please give it back to me. Stop hiding among the people and letting the wind carry your voice.

t_ch: 既然我知道是礼物——那自然要开个高价啦！风的子民可不会在一个地方停留太久，除非你的诚意能让风驻足。怎么说，嗯？
t_en: Now, now. Since I know it's a gift — That just means I need to milk its worth! The children of the wind never stay for long in one place, unless your offer is tempting enough to still the wind. What say you?

t_ch: 你要多少我给多少，开个价吧。
t_en: Name your price. I'll give you however much you want.

t_ch: 啊？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 咦？怎么了……
t_en: Hmm? What's wrong?

t_ch: 钱不需要可以捐给我…
t_en: If you don't want the money, I'd gladly take it off your hands...

t_ch: 啊…抱歉，我不擅长还价……
t_en: Ah... I'm sorry. Bargaining is really not my forte...
Pfft...

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈——「蜗居公主」的雅号，果然名不虚传——！
t_en: Hahaha! No wonder they call you Princess Homebody!

t_ch: 那两位，来我府上作客吧，换个更正式的场合谈生意。循着我播下的风，找到我的住处，没问题吧？
t_en: Tell you what, why don't you two come over to my place? It'll make a better venue for proper business discussions. You can find where I live through the winds I've sown, right?

t_ch: 能探到你的魂息就够了…轻而易举。
t_en: Piece of cake... I can do that even if I can only sense your soul.

t_ch: 好嘞，一言为定。啊，对了……
t_en: Alright, I'll see you there! Oh, by the way...

t_ch: 谈拢前，我先收个押金，没意见吧？嘿，走你——
t_en: Everyone cool with me collecting a deposit before we reach an agreement, right? Hehe. Now, come this way...

t_ch: <color=#eb4d3dff>背包中的「神血蜜露」被偷走了。</color>
t_en: <color=#eb4d3dff>The Dew of Divine Blood in the backpack has been stolen.</color>

t_ch: 这儿！看哪儿呢，在这边～
t_en: Over here, over here! ...No, not that way. This way!

t_ch: 我要真想藏起来，又何必当着你的面出现呢？
t_en: Come on. If I really wanted to hide, you'd never find me.

t_ch: 各位，别像只炸了毛的猫嘛，我也没那么恐怖吧，喵？
t_en: So, relax, you two! No need to get your hackles up. I'm not that scary, am I? Meow!

t_ch: 出现了！说话带「喵」的角色…
t_en: At last! A character that meows has appeared!

t_ch: 哈哈，怎么可能？千年以前没准还有人喜欢这口癖，现在早就不流行了。
t_en: Haha, no way! Some people might have been into that a millennium ago, but it's long since gone out of style.

t_ch: 什么鬼口癖——
t_en: Ew, what kind of quirk—

t_ch: 哈哈，瞧你那嫌弃样！干嘛啦，我就是表演一下——千年以前没准还有人喜欢这口癖，现在早就不流行了。
t_en: Whoa! That upset? Calm down. I'm just putting on a show with the meowing. Some people might have been into that a millennium ago, but it's long since gone out of style.

t_ch: 来吧，轻松点，来谈谈正事？
t_en: Loosen up, buddy! Shall we get down to business?

t_ch: 先把首饰还给我…否则一切免谈。
t_en: Give me the jewelry piece first... or everything's off the table.

t_ch: 还有我的神血蜜露！
t_en: And my Dew of Divine Blood!

t_ch: 礼物是首饰？
t_en: The gift is a piece of jewelry?

t_ch: 用料朴素，但做工一如既往地精湛。好久没见，手艺不减啊。而且…还特别用心。
t_en: The materials are nothing to wow at, but the workmanship is superb as always... You're still skilled as ever even after all this time. And you've put a lot of heart into this one... Hehe.

t_ch: …和你没关系。
t_en: ...Mind your own business.

t_ch: 哎哎，别这么剑拔弩张。放轻松，放轻松……
t_en: Oh, come now! Don't be so upset. Relax! Relax...

t_ch: 看！这地方视野不错，对吧？每个人在做什么、要去哪里，全都一览无余，还不会被人发现。
t_en: Look! Isn't the view here just great? You can see what everyone is doing and where they're going, and they won't even know you're here.

t_ch: 暖场也暖得差不多了，那咱们开门见山吧：方才只是开个玩笑，我邀请两位前来，主要是为了三件事……
t_en: Now that we've broken the ice, let's get to the point — Everything I've said so far has been a joke. I'm inviting you two here for three things:

t_ch: 一，裁缝女找到我，说希望我带你们前往斯缇科西亚，寻找死亡泰坦。
t_en: One, the seamstress found me and wants me to take you to Styxia to look for the Death Titan.

t_ch: 二…呵，元老院也找到我，让我确保{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的火种能被交到他们手中。
t_en: Two... Ha, the Council of Elders also found me and wants me to make sure the Coreflame of {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} ends up in their hands.

t_ch: 你这两边都不耽误啊。
t_en: Look at you! Having your finger in both pies.

t_ch: 想不到你面子这么大。
t_en: You're THAT famous?

t_ch: 那是。别看我低调，可是名副其实的半神。
t_en: Of course. I may keep a low profile, but I'm still a bona fide demigod.

t_ch: 裁缝女？
t_en: Seamstress?

t_ch: 金织、控制狂、奥赫玛第一美人、阿格莱雅妈妈…还要我多说几个外号吗？
t_en: Goldweaver, control freak, number one beauty of Okhema, Aglaea the Mom... How many more nicknames do you wanna hear?

t_ch: 最后，第三件事……
t_en: And last, but not least...

t_ch: 我找到二位，并告诉你们：以上两件事，我都不想干。
t_en: I'm telling you that I'm not interested in doing either of those things.

t_ch: 别去找什么死亡泰坦了——就这么简单。
t_en: Don't go look for the Death Titan. The end.

t_ch: 一段时间以前……
t_en: Some time ago...

t_ch: 嚯！这货可真够纯的……
t_en: Whoa, this is some high-quality stuff...

t_ch: 这一屋子叮呤咣啷的，得值多少钱啊…全是你靠「再创世」的噱头挣的？
t_en: And this room is full of it... How much are these worth, hmm? Did you get all of this through that Era Nova gimmick?

t_ch: 一些是家族财产，另一些来自改衣师的副业。别把逐火的事业与你惯常的勾当相提并论。
t_en: Some of it is family fortune, while the rest came from my business as a dressmaster. Don't liken the Flame-Chase work to your shady dealings.

t_ch: 好家伙…早知道我就撬你家大门去了，哪儿还犯得着流窜作案哪。
t_en: Wow... I should have busted down your door instead of going around like a thief.

t_ch: 说吧，好容易割下这么大一块肉给我，什么意思？
t_en: Well, spill. Why are you giving me so much money?

t_ch: 花钱请人办事。你值得这个价格。
t_en: Payment for doing work. You're worth this much.

t_ch: 你忘了？我应该表明过自己的态度吧。
t_en: Did you forget my stance to all this? I thought I made myself very clear.

t_ch: 逃避。逃避责任、逃避神谕、逃避命运——<color=#dbc291ff>「汝将与贪婪同行，亦将亡于分文。」</color>
t_en: You wish to run away. Run away from your responsibilities, from the prophecy, and from your destiny. <color=#dbc291ff>"You shall walk with greed, and die over petty change."</color>

t_ch: 当然，我明白，这道预言对你无比沉重，我也从未对「诡计」半神的逃避有所苛责。
t_en: I understand this prophecy weighs on you immensely. That is why I have never reproached the demigod of Trickery for running away.

t_ch: 但现在，我需要你。请你不要拒绝我的请求。
t_en: But I need you right now, so don't turn me down.
Hmm...

t_ch: 知道么，阿格莱雅？或许是神用金线掐住了你的喉咙，我从你的请求中听不出半点诚意。
t_en: You know, maybe the gods got your tongue with their golden threads, Aglaea. I'm not hearing any sincerity in your words.

t_ch: 给我一个让你低下高贵头颅的理由。
t_en: Give me a reason why you're bowing your noble head to me.

t_ch: …我们失去了缇安。
t_en: ...We lost Trianne.
Oh...

t_ch: 「门径」的神力已所剩无几。我需要另一位半神护送遐蝶，去完成她毕生之所求。
t_en: The divine power of Passages is running out. I need another demigod to escort Castorice and help her fulfill her lifelong wish.

t_ch: 噢，我可算是听明白了：你想让遐蝶白白送死，还想搭上我。
t_en: Oh, I get it now. You're sending Castorice to her death, and you want me to die with her.

t_ch: 这是遐蝶自己的请求。我从未见她如此坚决。
t_en: This is Castorice's own request. I have never seen her so determined.

t_ch: 她…主动提的？你没在诓我？
t_en: She... brought this up herself? You're not pulling my leg?

t_ch: 她也下定决心，要从布满迷雾的过去中走出。
t_en: She's also made up her mind to leave her confused past behind.

t_ch: 而你，赛飞儿：当你不再迷茫时，也必然会做出同样的选择。
t_en: ...As will you, Cipher, when you finally make up your mind.

t_ch: 话可别说的太死。时间让你我建起了城府，而她始终纯净得像朵花蕾……
t_en: Don't be so sure. Time has hardened the likes of you and I, but Castorice remains pure as driven snow.

t_ch: 也许她会想到牺牲的缇安，想到无数惨死的黄金裔，想到半截身子入土的你——最后一刻还没到呢，她到底想做什么，我还真没法替你乐观。
t_en: She can always turn back before the end, you know? Maybe she'll remember Trianne's sacrifice. Maybe she'll remember the countless martyred Chrysos Heirs. Maybe she'll remember you, with your one foot in the grave — I ain't as optimistic as you about what she'll want to do.

t_ch: 这样吧！不如先让我替你收着这一屋子宝贝，咱俩来打个赌吧？
t_en: Tell you what, why don't I take this room of treasures as a security deposit and we make a bet?

t_ch: 说说看。
t_en: Go on.

t_ch: 我赌她一定会打消这个念头。如果我输了，我不光会帮你做事，把这堆叮当响的好东西全数奉还，还会额外再送你一屋子满满当当的财宝，怎样？
t_en: I bet she'll change her mind and turn back. Now, if I lose, I'll do what you want me to do, return this deposit to you, plus giving you another roomful of treasures. How does that sound?

t_ch: 你说起话来眉飞色舞，仿佛稳操胜券。
t_en: You sound like you have all the confidence in the world.

t_ch: 既然如此，就照你说的，签订契约吧——并衷心希望你已做好了满盘皆输的准备。
t_en: In that case, let's make a pact with those conditions. I hope you're prepared to lose it all.

t_ch: 综上所述——
t_en: In conclusion...

t_ch: 我想赢，所以，我拜托两位别再惦记什么斯缇科西亚了。作为谢礼，我会把赢来的那一屋子叮呤咣啷分给各位，你们自己五五分账，或者怎么分，随便。
t_en: I want to win. So I hope you two can give up on going to Styxia. I'll give all the treasures in that room to you, and you can split it between yourselves however you want.

t_ch: 怎么说，很有诱惑力吧？
t_en: What do you think? Tempting, right?

t_ch: 我都快死了，要钱有什么用？
t_en: I'm almost dead. What use do I have for money?

t_ch: 容我回绝你的提议。如果我们无法找到死亡泰坦，{NICKNAME}的灵魂就会……
t_en: I decline. If we don't find the Death Titan, {NICKNAME}'s soul will...

t_ch: 翁法罗斯货币和我们的不一样。
t_en: The currency I use isn't the same as the one in Amphoreus.

t_ch: 哎呀灰子，我要给你的可不是人们几千年前买鱼用的破贝壳……
t_en: Oh, please! Dear Gray Mystery, I'm not giving you broken seashells people used in the markets millennia ago...

t_ch: 那可是黄金，足金，千足金！硬通货呀硬通货，你知道这三个字意味着什么吗？
t_en: I'm giving you gold! Pure, solid gold! It's a hard currency! You know what that means, right!?

t_ch: 不，这不是价值的问题。如果我们无法找到死亡泰坦，{NICKNAME}的灵魂就会……
t_en: It's not about the value of the reward. If we don't find the Death Titan, {NICKNAME}'s soul will...

t_ch: 好像也不是不行…
t_en: Maybe I should consider it...

t_ch: 这…这怎么能行呢！您的灵魂已几近消散了，阁下……
t_en: N—No! Your soul is almost gone for good...

t_ch: 再多的不便细说，你只需知道，斯缇科西亚我们非去不可。
t_en: I can't go into the details, but we have to go to Styxia.

t_ch: 我真有点看不懂了……
t_en: I really don't get how...

t_ch: 蜗居公主，虽然你一直都口口声声说，要找到{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}，解除自己的诅咒，除此之外什么都不关心……
t_en: Princess Homebody, you keep talking about needing to find {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}, about getting rid of your curse, and you don't care about anything else...

t_ch: 可你真的为自己活过，哪怕一天么？
t_en: But did you ever live for your own pleasure? Even for just one day?

t_ch: 你想说什么？
t_en: What are you trying to say?

t_ch: 我想说，阿格莱雅把你捡回来，可不是真让你为了她「再创世」的口号白白送死的。
t_en: I'm saying, contrary to your beliefs, Aglaea didn't take you in just so you can die for her Era Nova.

t_ch: 逐火之旅必定会伴随牺牲。若非如此，你也不必时时躲着阿格莱雅大人。
t_en: The Flame-Chase Journey walks hand in hand with sacrifices. Isn't that why you're always running away from Lady Aglaea?

t_ch: …嗐，算了，大家都有苦衷，我就不多嘴了。反正我也没打算兑现跟她的赌注，嘻。
t_en: Pah... Fine. Never mind. We all have our own problems. I ain't gonna say anything anymore. It's not like I was gonna honor that bet anyway, hehe.

t_ch: 不过，听我最后一句劝：斯缇科西亚可不是什么观光圣地。那地里埋着的玩意，就连十个…不，五十个阿格莱雅都拿不出手——
t_en: But listen to me: Styxia is no tourist destination. Sure, the treasures buried in there are ten... No, fifty times richer than Aglaea's vault —

t_ch: 可你知道我为什么从来没去过吗？因为就算我高低是个半神，都怕自己回不来。
t_en: Yet I've never been there and I never plan to! Because even a demigod can't be sure they can walk out of that place alive.

t_ch: 送你们过去？可以，得加钱。
t_en: And now I'm supposed to escort you to Styxia? Sure. Pay up.

t_ch: 你要多少，我给多少！
t_en: I'll give you however much you want!

t_ch: 我…我也可以。
t_en: M—Me too.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅已经替我付过了。
t_en: Aglaea has already paid for me.

t_ch: 她付的是金银财宝，给生者。你呢，要付的是「死者」的部分。
t_en: Her payment was in gold and precious stones, which only covers the living passenger. You need to pay me as a member of the dead.

t_ch: 虽然令人不快……
t_en: I don't like this, however...

t_ch: 扎格列斯半神的职责是敲竹杠？
t_en: Is ripping people off the authority of Zagreus' demigod?

t_ch: 您猜怎么着？还真是。或者说，这是我业务的一部分。
t_en: You know, you're not wrong. At least it's a part of my responsibilities.

t_ch: 虽然令人不快……
t_en: I don't like this, however...

t_ch: 我度过了比大部分人更漫长的时光，就算不像阿格莱雅大人那样善于营生，也还算有些积蓄。
t_en: I have lived a longer life than most people. Though I'm not as good at doing business as Lady Aglaea, I still have some savings.

t_ch: 我…可以把它们全都交给你。
t_en: I... I can give them all to you.

t_ch: 那你还过不过日子了？
t_en: Then how are you going to live the rest of your life?

t_ch: 那…那就百分之九十…不，八十吧……
t_en: Th—Then I'll give you ninety... no, eighty percent...

t_ch: 行吧，那我就不客气了：遐蝶八成的积蓄，外加一瓶神血蜜露…嘿，不算亏。
t_en: Well, don't mind if I do. Eighty percent of Castorice's savings and a bottle of Dew of Divine Blood... Heh, not a bad trade.

t_ch: 别忘了，把遐蝶的东西还给她。
t_en: Don't forget to give Castorice's jewelry back.

t_ch: 哎呀，知道啦，没想到灰子你这么记仇。
t_en: Okay, okay! I didn't know you hold grudges, Gray Mystery.

t_ch: 等我满血复活你就知错了。
t_en: I'll show you who's boss once I've recovered with full HP!

t_ch: 哎呀，人家好怕怕…嘻，能追上我的尾巴再说吧？
t_en: Waaaah, I'm so scared! Tee-hee, can you even catch my tail?

t_ch: 喏，接着——！
t_en: Here, catch!

t_ch: 呀，轻、轻点…！
t_en: Ah! Be careful with that...!

t_ch: 不过，计划有变：明天别去浴场，来奥赫玛城门口找我。
t_en: A minor change in plans, though. Don't go to the bath tomorrow. Come look for me at Okhema's city gates instead.

t_ch: 更换接头地的原因是……
t_en: So why are you changing the meeting place...?

t_ch: 没什么，单纯因为我怕了裁缝女那副冷冰冰的面孔。如果可以，我一秒都不想在奥赫玛多待。
t_en: Oh, nothing really. Just a bit scared of the seamstress' frosty face. I wouldn't stay a second longer in Okhema if it were up to me.

t_ch: 好了，交易结束，我来捎各位一程吧——
t_en: Well, now that we've struck a deal, why don't I give you guys a lift back?

t_ch: ——天高路远，先睡个好觉吧，两位。
t_en: It's gonna be a long ride, so have a good rest!
Ahhhh...!!!

t_ch: 次日 奥赫玛
t_en: Next day, Okhema...

t_ch: （这是哪儿…我的房间吗？我怎么回来的？）
t_en: (Where am I...? Is this my room? How did I get back here?)

t_ch: （硬币…赛飞儿？她还帮我把被子盖好了？！）
t_en: (This is... a coin...? It was Cipher? And she even tucked me in!?)

t_ch: （算了…时间不早了，出发吧。）
t_en: (Never mind... It's getting late. I'd better get going.)

t_ch: …还可以靠人家哦！
t_en: Don't worry. Mem's here to help!

t_ch: 原来你也在！
t_en: So you came!

t_ch: 你还会瞬间移动？
t_en: Can you teleport too?

t_ch: 毕竟人家不止外表可爱，还是实力派呢。
t_en: Being cute isn't my only forte. I'm strong, too!

t_ch: 迷迷，你交钱了吗？
t_en: Mem, did you pay your fare?

t_ch: 啊，这个…人家小小一只，身高不够，可以免票吧？
t_en: Um... I don't think I even meet the height requirement for the ticket, so I won't need one, right?

t_ch: 这里就是「斯缇科西亚」，巨龙与海浪的城邦…依照老师的猜想，不知去向的{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}就隐藏在这片废墟中。
t_en: This is Styxia, the city of dragons and waves... Anaxa hypothesizes that the missing {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} is somewhere in these ruins.

t_ch: 这…就是它的诞生之地，或长眠之地。
t_en: This is the land of their birth... Or perhaps of their death.

t_ch: 有更多线索吗？
t_en: Are there any other clues?

t_ch: 该怎么找到它？
t_en: How would we find them?

t_ch: 这题我会，落叶归根。
t_en: Aha, I know this one! They returned whence they came!

t_ch: 老师曾用炼金秘法，成功将自己的灵魂与{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}、{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}短暂熔合，又在弥留之际受到{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}招引，窥见了流淌的冥河。
t_en: Anaxa had used a secret alchemical art to temporarily fuse his soul with {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}. And when he was on the verge of death, he was pulled by {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} and caught a glimpse of the flowing River of Souls.

t_ch: 被称作「渎神之举」的炼成，也许能使凡人的灵魂与泰坦相互吸引……
t_en: This alchemy, considered a sacrilegious act, might actually make the souls of mortals and Titans attract each other...

t_ch: 尽管{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}在过去的千年几乎杳无音讯，也从未被信徒寻获。但那引力…或许能将它召唤至此。
t_en: Even though {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} has been missing for a thousand years and even their followers haven't been able to find them... perhaps that special attraction could summon them here.

t_ch: 噢！所以，我们要用那块「贤者之石」，在这里把泰坦给…变出来？
t_en: Oh! So are we going to use that Philosopher's Stone to... conjure the Titan?

t_ch: 想必是这样了。只不过，眼下的线索仅有这座城邦的名字，还有巨龙童话……
t_en: I guess that's the only way. The only leads we have now are the name of this city and the fairy tale about the dragon...

t_ch: {RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}，真是比人家还神秘呢……
t_en: {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} is even more mysterious than I am...

t_ch: 是啊，老师的智慧也只能照亮我们脚下这方寸土地。只要踏出第一步，前路就是未知的浓雾…但在我漫长的旅行中，这也不是第一次了。
t_en: Indeed. Even one as erudite as Anaxa could only point us to this place, and no further. We will be entering the unknown as soon as we take our first steps... But that won't be the first time in my long journeys.

t_ch: 我相信……
t_en: I believe that...

t_ch: 比起犹豫，还是启程更好。也许就在今天，命运会恻隐一时，抛下些许仁慈。
t_en: Rather than standing still, taking action is by far the better option. Perhaps fate might take pity on us today and show some compassion.

t_ch: 阁下…还好吧？
t_en: Are you feeling alright?

t_ch: 好晕…她真的没有违章超速吗…
t_en: Ugh... I swear she was speeding...

t_ch: 给我带哪了，还是翁法罗斯吗？
t_en: Where am I? Are we still in Amphoreus?

t_ch: 她是「诡计」的半神，当她将「翻飞之币」高高抛起，便会获得无可比拟的神速，来去只在一瞬间。
t_en: She's the demigod of Trickery. When she flips the Coin of Whimsy into the air, she gains incredible speed that lets her travel anywhere in an instant.

t_ch: 就像现在，她似乎没有多待一秒的打算…接下来的路，看来只能靠我们自己了。
t_en: ...Just like how she clearly had no intention of lingering here for even a second longer. We can only depend on ourselves for the rest of the way.

t_ch: 哎呀！艳阳高照，真是正当离别的时刻哪。
t_en: Mmm! Bright and sunny, just the weather for farewells.

t_ch: 你来了…阁下。
t_en: You're... here.

t_ch: 真就咱们仨？
t_en: Really? Just the three of us?

t_ch: 当然，三人一组可是挺进决赛圈的最佳配置。
t_en: Totally. The trio team is the best set up to reach the finals.

t_ch: 我得跟阿格莱雅打个招呼…
t_en: I'll go give Aglaea a heads-up...

t_ch: 不用惦记了，我跟她交代过——她没意见。
t_en: No need. I already told her. She's fine with it.

t_ch: 啧啧，遐蝶…你可真是会给我出难题呀。想带着你一起旅行，还不能真的触碰到你，真麻烦呀。
t_en: Tsk tsk, you sure know how to put me in a spot, Castorice... I need to travel with you, but I can't touch you. What a bother.

t_ch: 抱歉……
t_en: Sorry...

t_ch: 逗逗你，别放在心上。
t_en: Aw, I was just teasing. Don't take it to heart.

t_ch: 不过呢，二位知道吗？依照神话故事里说的，想要让死者顺利渡过冥河，去往冥界，就得在他们的嘴里，或者眼睛上放上一枚{RUBY_B#小银币}欧布罗斯{RUBY_E#}……
t_en: Did you know that the legends say you have to put an {RUBY_B#little silver coin}obol{RUBY_E#} into the mouth of the dead or over their eyes if you want them to successfully cross the River of Souls and enter the nether realm?

t_ch: 这是通往冥界的摆渡费用。但这次就不真的问你们要了，我垫付，咱们做个样子就行。
t_en: That's the ferry fare for entering the nether realm. However, I honestly won't ask you to pay this time. I'll pay. And then we'll just have to go through the motions...

t_ch: 你还挺会做顺水人情。
t_en: You sure are helpful when it doesn't cost you anything.

t_ch: 哈，只是为了给这趟旅程增加些仪式感，别太放在心上。
t_en: Hey, I'm just trying to make this journey feel more special, okay? Don't read too much into it.

t_ch: 我们又不是没付过！
t_en: We already paid you so much!

t_ch: 一码归一码，这次只是为了给这趟旅程增加些仪式感。
t_en: Hey, that was a different purchase. Look, I'm just trying to make this journey feel more special.

t_ch: 你到底敲了遐蝶多少钱？
t_en: Just how much have you swindled Castorice of?

t_ch: 没事，没多少……
t_en: Don't worry... it's not a lot...

t_ch: 好咯～直达冥界的捷足之旅，准备启程！硬币给我，我要发力了。
t_en: Alright! Pass me the coin — it's time to get the god's powers going! A fleet-footed trip to the nether realm, here we come!

t_ch: 啊，对了！走之前，最后送给两位一句忠告。尤其是给你的，蜗居公主——
t_en: Oh, one last thing before we go. I gotta give you this piece of advice, especially to you, Princess Homebody —

t_ch: 风是等不来的。难得来人间走一遭，别白活了呀。
t_en: You can't catch the wind by waiting. You only live once, so make the most of every moment you have.

t_ch: （丹恒还没回来啊……）
t_en: (Dan Heng isn't back yet, huh...)

t_ch: 阁下…是我。
t_en: ...It's me.

t_ch: 看来对「暂停逐火之旅」的全民表决已经开始了…好多人。
t_en: The streets are packed... It seems the vote to pause the Flame-Chase Journey has begun.

t_ch: 记住我的教诲，动手吧。
t_en: It is time. Remember what I taught you, child.

t_ch: 不，别杀我……
t_en: No... Please don't kill me...

t_ch: 刽子手
t_en: You executioner!

t_ch: 杀人凶手
t_en: You murderer!

t_ch: 为什么
t_en: Why!?

t_ch: 怪物
t_en: Monster!

t_ch: 圣…圣女……
t_en: H—Holy Maiden...

t_ch: 去吧，为她带去光荣的死亡。
t_en: Go. Grant her glorious death.

t_ch: 谢…谢…你……
t_en: Th—Thank... you...

t_ch: 动手吧，刽子手……
t_en: Do it then! Executioner...

t_ch: 你听到他说的话了。
t_en: You heard him.
